ground_truth.title: How Many Companies Use Cloud Computing in 2025? [10 Statistics and Insights] | Edge Delta
ground_truth.author: Edge Delta Team
ground_truth.main_content: Cloud computing has become a significant factor in streamlining business operations. It provides services like compute, storage, databases, cybersecurity, analytics, AI-powered cloud products, and more over the Internet. It offers faster innovation, economies of scale, and flexible resources, so many companies are integrating it. Over 90% of companies worldwide already use cloud services—including AWS, Google, and Microsoft. The number of cloud computing users will grow significantly in the coming years. Explore more about cloud computing by looking at its statistics. Discover how many companies use cloud computing, its market size, and more. Editor's Choice: 94% of all companies worldwide use cloud computing in their operations. Forbes Global has generated an EBITDA value of over $3 trillion. Market drivers for public cloud computing have increased to 23.8%. AWS dominated the market with a 32% cloud market share. 85% of organizations are projected to embrace a cloud-first principle by 2025. 73% of the insurance sector uses hybrid architectures. Global Cloud Computing garnered $331 billion, projected to grow by 23%. 97% of IT leaders are planning to expand their cloud systems. Public cloud end-user spending is projected to increase by 20.7% worldwide. By the Numbers: Company Use of Cloud Computing in 2024: A Cloud Security Alliance report revealed that 98% of organizations worldwide use cloud services. Recent data indicates a significant rise in cloud usage yearly. A 2022 study with 753 technical and business professionals worldwide reported that 63% of the respondents used the cloud 'heavily.' The use of cloud services was 59% in 2021 and 53% in 2020. Top Trends and Insights Shaping the Cloud Computing Market: The global cloud computing market has significantly increased to $446.51 billion in 2022 and garnered $500 billion in 2023. It is expected to surpass $1 trillion by 2028 and $1.6 trillion by 2030. The latest report predicts the market will grow at a significant CAGR of 17.43% through 2032. 94% of companies worldwide are adopting cloud computing. In 2023, corporate data garnered 60% of data stored in the cloud. Cloud computing adoption will generate over $3 trillion by 2030. In 2023, cloud computing market drivers for public cloud increased to 23.8%. Amazon Web Services dominated the cloud computing key players with a 32% market share. Unveiling Surprising Cloud Computing Adoption Statistics: Globally, businesses use public, private, and hybrid clouds. According to Gartner, more than 85% of organizations will embrace a cloud-first principle by 2025. Hybrid cloud is the top choice for the insurance industry, with 73% adoption. 22% of companies use public cloud computing services. 89% of companies use a multi-cloud approach. Traditional SMBs are less likely to use cloud computing services. Public cloud end-user spending is projected to increase by 20.7% worldwide. AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud obtained total spending of 66% in Q4 2023. Wrap Up: Cloud computing has transformed business operations while offering unparalleled opportunities for growth and innovation. The statistics above highlight the widespread adoption of cloud services, the immense growth of the cloud market, and the numerous benefits to various industries of cloud computing.
with: Over 90% of companies worldwide already use cloud services—including AWS, Google, and Microsoft.
94% of all companies worldwide use cloud computing in their operations.
A Cloud Security Alliance report revealed that 98% of organizations worldwide use cloud services.
Amazon Web Services dominated the market with a 32% cloud market share.
89% of companies use a multi-cloud approach.
